title: 10 best herbal capsules for itchy eyes

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree capsules helps with itchy eyes because they contain natural anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm irritated eye tissues. The capsules' extract of maidenhair tree, also known as Ginkgo biloba, has been traditionally used to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the eyes. As a result, it provides effective relief from itchy, red, and watery eyes caused by allergies, dryness, or other environmental factors.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidants help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, which can contribute to itchiness and discomfort.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The herb's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, can reduce swelling and irritation in the eyes, providing relief from itchy sensations.

Immunomodulatory effects: Ginkgo biloba capsules may help regulate the immune response in the eyes, reducing the severity of allergic reactions that can cause itchiness and other uncomfortable symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and irritation in the eyes, providing relief from itchy eyes.

Bilobalide: This triterpene has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals and reduce inflammation, leading to relief from itchy eyes.
Quercetin: This flavonoid has potent anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, which help reduce allergic reactions and inflammation in the eyes, providing relief from itchy eyes.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the primary source of the active compounds used in Ginkgo biloba supplements, including flavonoids and terpenoids, which help to soothe itchy eyes.
Seeds: The seeds of Ginkgo biloba are another commonly used part, as they contain the flavonoid bilobalide, which has anti-inflammatory properties that may help alleviate itchy eyes.
Barks: The barks of the Ginkgo biloba tree are also used, as they contain flavonoids and terpenoids that may help to reduce inflammation and itching in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 500g of ginkgo biloba leaves and dry them in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 2 hours.
Grind the dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 10 grams of the ginkgo biloba powder with 30 grams of gelatin in a bowl.
Add 10 milliliters of distilled water to the bowl and heat the mixture in a double boiler at 80°c for 10 minutes.
Fill gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them using a capsule filler machine.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eyebright capsules helps with itchy eyes because they contain a potent blend of herbs that soothe and calm irritated ocular tissues. The gentle, anti-inflammatory properties of eyebright help to reduce redness and swelling, while its natural antibacterial and antiviral agents combat infections that may be causing the itchiness. Additionally, eyebright's mucilaginous texture helps to lubricate and moisturize the eyes, providing long-lasting relief from itching and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Eyebright's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce swelling and itching in the eyes by reducing the production of inflammatory chemicals.
Antihistamine property: The antihistamine compounds in Eyebright capsules counteract the effects of histamines, which can cause itchiness, redness, and watering of the eyes.

Anti-allergic property: Eyebright's anti-allergic compounds help alleviate allergic reactions that can lead to itchy eyes by inhibiting the release of chemical mediators involved in allergic responses.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds, especially euphrasin and eupalutin, have anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties that help reduce itching and swelling in the eyes.
Flavonoids (e.g., quercetin, kaempferol): Flavonoids in Euphrasia officinalis have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which help alleviate redness, itching, and irritation in the eyes.

Triterpenoids (e.g., oleanolic acid, ursolic acid): These compounds possess anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties that help soothe and calm the eyes, reducing itching and discomfort associated with eye conditions.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain bioactive compounds that help to reduce inflammation and alleviate itchy eyes.
Flowers: They are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, useful in soothing itchy eyes.
Seeds: They contain euphrasine, a compound that helps to reduce inflammation and itching associated with conjunctivitis and other eye conditions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest euphrasia officinalis flowers and leaves in early morning after dew has evaporated using scissors or a sharp knife.
Dry the harvested plant material in a single layer at 40°c for 2 hours using a temperature-controlled oven.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a high-speed blender or a coffee grinder for 10 minutes.
Mix the ground powder with a binding agent such as gelatin or cellulose in a ratio of 1:1 using a spoon.
Fill the mixed powder into gelatin capsules using a capsule filling machine or a syringe for 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold capsules helps with itchy eyes because they contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm irritated eyes. The herbs in these capsules, such as calendula, work to reduce redness and swelling, while also combating free radicals that can exacerbate itchiness. Additionally, the capsules' natural antihistamine properties help to alleviate itching caused by allergies or other irritants, providing quick relief for itchy, uncomfortable eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Calendula's anti-inflammatory properties can reduce redness and swelling in the eyes, providing relief from itchy, irritated eyes.
Antibacterial: The herb's antibacterial properties may help combat underlying eye infections or irritants that cause itchiness, reducing discomfort and promoting healing.
Emollient: Calendula's emollient properties can soothe and moisturize the delicate skin around the eyes, calming itchy, dry, or irritated areas.
remedy_constituents: Sesquiterpenes: They help reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes, which can contribute to itchy eyes by alleviating the underlying cause of the discomfort.

Flavonoids (specifically, quercetin and kaempferol): They possess potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help soothe and calm the irritated eyes, reducing the sensation of itchiness.

Triterpenoids (specifically, oleanolic acid and ursolic acid): They have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help combat infections and reduce inflammation in the eyes, alleviating itchiness caused by infections or allergies.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are used to make capsules for itchy eyes because they contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Leaves: The leaves are used to make capsules for itchy eyes because they possess antimicrobial properties that can help reduce eye infections and inflammation.
Seeds: The seeds are used to make capsules for itchy eyes because they contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce eye redness and swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh calendula officinalis flowers during peak bloom in dry sunny weather.
Clean the flowers by gently shaking off any dirt and debris from the plant material.
Dry the flowers using a dehydrator at 40c for 6 hours or by air drying for 7 days.
Grind 30g of dried calendula officinalis flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Fill 100 gelatin capsules with 250mg of the powder and seal them using a capsule filler for 5 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower capsules helps with itchy eyes because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated eye tissues. The herb's antioxidant compounds also help to reduce redness and swelling, while its antimicrobial agents work to combat infections that can cause itchiness. By addressing the underlying causes of itchy eyes, purple coneflower capsules provide long-lasting relief from discomfort, allowing for a smoother and more comfortable vision experience.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Purple coneflower contains anti-inflammatory compounds like caffeic acid, which helps reduce inflammation and itching in the eyes caused by allergies or irritants.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in purple coneflower, such as flavonoids, help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, reducing the severity of itchy eye symptoms.

Antihistamine-like effects: Purple coneflower's active compounds, like quercetin, have been shown to inhibit histamine release, which can help alleviate itchy eyes caused by allergic reactions or hay fever.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds, particularly echinacoside and echinulin, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce itching and inflammation in itchy eyes.

Alkaloids (specifically Cichoric Acid and Chlorogenic Acid): As phenolic compounds, these alkaloids possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate itchiness and redness associated with itchy eyes.

Flavonoids (specifically Quercetin): Quercetin, a flavonoid present in Echinacea purpurea, has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help soothe itchy eyes and reduce inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Echinacea purpurea are often used due to their high content of alkylamides, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory properties that may help alleviate itchy eyes.

Leaves: The leaves are commonly used due to their high concentration of caffeic acid derivatives, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help soothe itchy eyes.

Flowers: The flowers of Echinacea purpurea are frequently used due to their high content of flavonoids, such as kaempferol and quercetin, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help alleviate itchy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature echinacea purpurea flowers and roots from a clean and organic garden when they are fully ripe.
Dry the harvested flowers and roots in a warm and well-ventilated area for 1 to 2 weeks.
Crush the dried flowers and roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or a coffee grinder.
Mix the powdered echinacea purpurea with a binding agent such as gelatin or starch in a 10:1 ratio by weight.
Fill gelatin capsules with the echinacea purpurea mixture and seal them immediately to preserve potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder capsules helps with itchy eyes because they contain natural anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe irritated eye tissues. The antioxidants and flavonoids present in these capsules reduce inflammation and alleviate dryness, which are common causes of itchy eyes. Additionally, the anti-allergic properties of elder capsules help to calm down allergic reactions that can cause itching and redness. By providing a soothing and calming effect on the eyes, elder capsules offer natural relief from itchy eyes.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Elderberry's antioxidant properties help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes, which can contribute to itchiness and discomfort.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids present in elderberry capsules have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and redness associated with itchy eyes.
Antiviral properties: Elderberry's antiviral properties can help combat viral conjunctivitis (pink eye), a common cause of itchy eyes, by inhibiting the replication of viruses that cause infection.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids (Quercetin, Kaempferol, and Isoquercetin): These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and itching in the eyes by inhibiting the release of histamine, a chemical responsible for allergic reactions.

Phenolic Acids (Chlorogenic Acid and Caffeic Acid): These antioxidants can help neutralize free radicals and reduce inflammation, which may alleviate itchy eyes by minimizing oxidative stress and promoting healing.

Saponins (Sambunigrin and Sambunine): These glycosides have anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, which can help soothe itchy eyes by reducing histamine release and alleviating allergic responses.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their high content of flavonoids and anthocyanins, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe itchy eyes.
Leaves: They are used due to their flavonoid content, particularly quercetin, which is known for its anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties that help alleviate itchy eyes.
Fruits: They are used due to their high content of flavonoids and anthocyanins, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm itchy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-40 grams of sambucus nigra flowers and leaves at peak bloom time typically late spring and early summer.
Dry the harvested flowers and leaves in a low-temperature dehydrator at 40°c for 2-3 hours.
Use a coffee grinder to finely grind 10 grams of dried sambucus nigra flowers and leaves.
Mix 10% gelatin with 90% distilled water in a saucepan and heat until the gelatin dissolves.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 250-300 milligrams of the finely ground sambucus nigra powder and seal.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion capsules helps with itchy eyes because they contain anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm irritated eyes. The capsules' rich source of antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals helps to reduce inflammation and redness in the eyes, alleviating itchiness and discomfort. Additionally, dandelion's natural antibacterial properties help to prevent infections that can cause itchy eyes. As a result, taking dandelion capsules regularly can provide long-term relief from itchy eyes and promote overall eye health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Dandelion's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce redness, swelling, and irritation in itchy eyes, providing relief from discomfort.
Antioxidant: The antioxidant compounds in dandelion capsules can neutralize free radicals that can contribute to eye irritation, reducing oxidative stress and promoting overall eye health.
Anti-allergic: Dandelion's anti-allergic properties can help alleviate allergic reactions that cause itchiness, redness, and tearing in the eyes by reducing histamine release and inflammation.
remedy_constituents: Furanocoumarins: Help reduce inflammation and itching in the eyes due to their anti-inflammatory and anti-oxidant properties.
Flavonoids (especially quercetin): Act as natural antihistamines, reducing inflammation and itching in the eyes by blocking histamine release and stabilizing mast cells.
Lignans (especially taraxasterol): Exert anti-inflammatory and anti-allergic effects, which may help alleviate itchy eyes by reducing swelling and inflammation in the conjunctiva.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Known for their anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Flowers: Rich in flavonoids and other compounds that have anti-allergic and anti-inflammatory effects, reducing itchy and watery eyes.
Roots: Contain saponins and other bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, helping to alleviate eye irritations.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20 fresh dandelion roots in the morning to ensure optimal potency and dry them thoroughly for 2 hours using a food dehydrator.
Grind 2 cups of dried dandelion roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2 minutes at high speed.
Mix 1/2 cup of the powdered dandelion root with 1/2 cup of gelatin capsules in a bowl and stir for 5 minutes to combine evenly.
Fill 100 gelatin capsules with the mixture using a capsule filler and tighten the caps tightly for 1 minute.
Label 50 capsules with the date and contents using a permanent marker and store them in an airtight container for 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort capsules helps with itchy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The herb contains bioactive compounds that reduce inflammation in the body, which is often the underlying cause of itchy eyes. When taken as a supplement, St John's Wort capsules can help alleviate dryness and irritation in the eyes, providing relief from itchy sensations. Additionally, its antioxidant properties can also protect the eyes from further damage caused by free radicals. As a natural remedy, St John's Wort capsules offer a safe and effective way to soothe itchy eyes.
remedy_properties: Hyperforin: This compound has anti-inflammatory and antihistamine effects, which can help reduce itching and redness in the eyes caused by allergic reactions or inflammation.
Hypericin: This pigment has antimicrobial properties, which can help combat bacterial, viral, or fungal infections that may be contributing to itchy eyes, such as conjunctivitis or blepharitis.

Flavonoids: The flavonoid compounds present in St John's Wort have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, alleviating itchiness and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: Hyperforin is a phenolic compound that has anti-inflammatory and antihistaminic properties, which help to reduce the inflammation and itching in itchy eyes.
Quercetin: Quercetin is a flavonoid that has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and calm itchy eyes by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress.

Naphthodianthrone derivatives (e.g. hyperchrome): Naphthodianthrone derivatives are a class of compounds that have anti-inflammatory and immunosuppressive properties, which help to reduce the production of pro-inflammatory mediators and alleviate itchy eyes.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Hypericum perforatum are often used in herbal remedies because they contain high amounts of flavonoids and naphthoquinones, which have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
Flowers: The flowers of Hypericum perforatum are commonly used due to their high concentration of flavonoids and essential oils, which help soothe and calm irritated skin and eyes.

Seeds: The seeds of Hypericum perforatum are sometimes used in herbal capsules due to their content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds, which may help reduce inflammation and alleviate itchy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Gather 40 grams of dried hypericum perforatum flowers and 20 grams of gelatin powder for the capsule shells.
Weigh 10 grams of the dried flowers and mix with 5 grams of gelatin powder.
Add 100 milliliters of distilled water to the mixture and let it steep for 30 minutes to an hour.
Strain the liquid mixture and add 5 grams of gelatin powder to 100 milliliters of distilled water.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the liquid mixture and seal them immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain capsules helps with itchy eyes because they contain anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated ocular tissues. The plant's natural compounds, such as cinnamic acid and aucubin, work to reduce redness and swelling, while also providing a cooling effect on the eyes. This relief from inflammation allows for reduced itchiness and discomfort, making it an effective natural remedy for individuals suffering from itchy or irritated eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: Plantain capsules contain compounds that reduce inflammation and swelling in the eye, which helps to alleviate itchy eyes caused by allergic reactions or irritants.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in Plantain capsules help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to itchiness and discomfort.

Soothing and calming effects: The mucilages (gelling substances) in Plantain capsules create a protective barrier on the eye surface, soothing and calming itchy, irritated eyes and providing relief from discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins in Plantago major help with itchy eyes by reducing inflammation and soothing the mucous membranes in the eyes.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids, particularly quercetin, in Plantago major act as antioxidants and anti-inflammatory agents, helping to alleviate itchy eyes caused by allergies or irritation.
Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids in Plantago major have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce eye irritation and itching by preventing infections and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
Seeds: They are used for their mucilaginous properties, which can help to reduce itching and inflammation in the eyes.
Stems: They are used due to their ability to provide a soothing and calming effect, which can help to alleviate itchy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh plantago major leaves on a sunny morning in late summer.
Clean and dry the harvested leaves in a warm oven at 50°c for 2 hours.
Grind 10g of dried plantago major leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered plantago major with 100g of gelatin to create a uniform capsule filling.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the prepared plantago major mixture and seal them immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow capsules helps with itchy eyes because they contain mucilages, which are natural soothing agents that calm irritated tissues. When taken orally, these mucilages are absorbed into the bloodstream and transported to the eyes, where they provide relief from dryness, itchiness, and inflammation. The herbs in marshmallow capsules work by forming a protective barrier on the surface of the eyes, reducing discomfort and promoting healthy tear production. This natural approach can bring much-needed relief for individuals experiencing chronic itchy eye symptoms.
remedy_properties: Mucilage: Marshmallow mucilage forms a protective barrier on the surface of the eye, soothing and calming irritated tissues and reducing itchiness by coating and moisturizing the affected area.
Anti-inflammatory compounds: Marshmallow contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and saponins that help reduce redness and swelling in the eyes, thereby relieving itching and discomfort.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in marshmallow capsules help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to eye irritation and inflammation, further reducing itchiness and promoting overall eye health.
remedy_constituents: Mucilages: Mucilages, such as althaea mucilage, help with itchy eyes by forming a soothing film on the surface of the eyes, reducing inflammation and providing relief from itchiness.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, help with itchy eyes by reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms associated with allergic reactions, such as itchiness and redness.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, such as gallic acid and caffeic acid, help with itchy eyes by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce itchiness and soothe the eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used due to their high mucilage content, which provides soothing and protective properties for itchy eyes.
Leaves: Utilized for their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which help to calm itchy eyes and reduce discomfort.
Barks: Employed for their astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and soothe itchy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest althaea officinalis roots after 2 years of cultivation and dry them in a well-ventilated area for 2 weeks.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 30 minutes.
Sift the powder through a 60-mesh sieve to remove any lumps and impurities for 20 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 300-500 mg of the sifted powder using a capsule filler machine for 10 minutes.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dry place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile capsules helps with itchy eyes because of its natural anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. When taken orally, chamomile's bioactive compounds are absorbed into the bloodstream, where they can effectively reduce inflammation and irritation in the eyes. This leads to a calming effect on itchy eyes, providing relief from redness, itchiness, and discomfort caused by dry eyes, allergies, or environmental factors. As a result, chamomile capsules offer a natural and gentle solution for individuals seeking to alleviate itchy eye issues.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory property helps to reduce redness, swelling, and itching in the eyes, providing relief from irritated and itchy eyes.

Antihistamine property: Chamomile's antihistamine properties can help to counteract the release of histamines, which contribute to itchy, watery eyes, providing rapid relief from allergic reactions or environmental irritants.
Sothing and calming property: Chamomile's soothing and calming effects can help to calm irritated eye tissues, reducing itchiness and promoting relaxation, allowing for more comfortable vision.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: An apigenin is a type of flavonoid that has anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, which help to reduce itching and inflammation in the eyes.
Luteolin: Luteolin is another flavonoid found in Matricaria chamomilla that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to soothe and calm itchy eyes.
Chamazulene: Chamazulene is a sesquiterpene lactone that has anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, which help to reduce itching, redness, and swelling in the eyes.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are the primary part used due to their high concentration of flavonoids, particularly apigenin, which has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that help relieve itchy eyes.
Seeds: While less commonly used, Matricaria chamomilla seeds also contain apigenin and other beneficial compounds that can help reduce inflammation and provide relief from itchy eyes.
Leaves:
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers during full bloom and dry them thoroughly in the shade for 7 days.
Grind 30 grams of dried flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix 2 teaspoons of the powder with 2 teaspoons of cornstarch and 1 teaspoon of tapioca powder.
Fill gelatin capsules with the powder mixture using a capsule filler and seal them immediately.
Store the capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe capsules helps with itchy eyes because they contain a potent anti-inflammatory compound called aloin, which soothes irritated eyes and reduces itchiness. The capsule's aloe vera extract also has antiseptic properties that help to calm redness and discomfort caused by allergies or irritants. Additionally, the vitamins and minerals in the capsule support overall eye health, promoting healthy tear production and reducing dryness that can contribute to itchy eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal aloe capsules contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce redness and swelling associated with itchy eyes, providing relief from discomfort.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in herbal aloe capsules can help neutralize free radicals that may be contributing to the inflammation and irritation causing itchiness in the eyes.
Soothing and calming properties: Herbal aloe capsules contain mucilages and other soothing compounds that can calm irritated eyes, reduce itchiness, and promote healing of any underlying conditions.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Roots: Containing bioactive compounds like aloetic acid, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate itchiness.
remedy_recipe: Collect high-quality aloe vera gel and fill a sterile container with 500 milliliters of pure water.
Extract the bioactive compounds from the aloe vera gel by mixing it with 250 milliliters of vodka for two hours.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth to remove the solids and filter it using a coffee filter paper.
Dry the filtered liquid using a vacuum drying machine at 50 degrees celsius for four hours and then grind into fine powder.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with one gram of aloe vera powder per capsule and store in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsules helps with itchy eyes because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. When taken orally, the glycyrrhizin is absorbed into the bloodstream and reaches the eyes, where it helps to reduce inflammation and relieve itchiness. Additionally, the flavonoids present in licorice capsules have antioxidant properties that help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, further contributing to their soothing effects on itchy eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal licorice capsules contain flavonoids and triterpenes, which help to reduce inflammation and swelling in the eye, providing relief from itchiness and discomfort.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in herbal licorice capsules help to neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and damage to the eyes, contributing to itchy eyes.

Anti-allergic properties: Herbal licorice capsules contain compounds like glycyrrhizin, which have anti-inflammatory and anti-allergic effects, helping to alleviate itching, redness, and irritation caused by allergic reactions.
remedy_parts: Roots: Licorice roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and swelling in itchy eyes, making them an effective ingredient in medicinal capsules.
Barks: The bark of the licorice plant contains glycyrrhizin, a compound that has soothing and calming effects on irritated eye tissues, providing relief from itchiness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for about 10 minutes.
Mix the powdered root with 100-200 ml of boiling distilled water in a heat-proof bowl to create an infusion, allowing it to steep for 15-20 minutes.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container, discarding the solids and setting aside any excess liquid for future use.
Combine the strained liquid with 100-200 ml of glycerin and mix well, allowing it to cool and thicken at room temperature for about 2 hours.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 250-500 mg of the cooled mixture, sealing them tightly and storing them in an airtight container for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Beach rose capsules helps with itchy eyes because they contain natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated eyes. The herb's extracts work to reduce redness and swelling, while its antihistamine-like effects combat itchiness caused by allergens or irritants. Additionally, beach rose capsules may also help to reduce the production of histamine, a chemical released in response to allergic reactions, further alleviating itching and discomfort. As a result, users can enjoy relief from itchy eyes and a more comfortable vision experience.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Beach Rose capsules contain flavonoids and anthocyanins, which have anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce swelling and redness in the eyes caused by itchiness.
Antioxidant property: The capsules' high antioxidant content helps protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate itchy eyes and dryness.

Antihistamine property: Beach Rose contains histamine-reducing compounds that help alleviate itching and irritation in the eyes by blocking the release of histamine, a chemical released during allergic reactions.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the beach rose plant contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce redness and swelling in itchy eyes.
Barks: The barks of the beach rose plant have antiseptic properties that help prevent infections and promote healing in itchy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried rosa rugosa petals and pure silica gel capsules of size 000 or 0.
Measure 20 grams of dried rosa rugosa petals and sift through a fine-mesh sieve to remove any debris.
Mix the sifted rosa rugosa petals with 10 grams of glycerin in a small bowl until a uniform paste forms.
Fill each silica gel capsule with approximately 250 milligrams of the rosa rugosa-paste mixture and seal tightly.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild potato capsules helps with itchy eyes because they contain anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated eyes. The herbal extract in these capsules has natural antihistamine and antibacterial agents that reduce redness, itchiness, and swelling associated with eye irritations. Additionally, the antioxidants present in wild potato capsules help protect the delicate tissues of the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, further reducing discomfort and promoting overall eye health and comfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal wild potato capsules help reduce inflammation in the eyes, which can cause itchiness and discomfort by soothing and calming irritated tissues.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant compounds present in these capsules neutralize free radicals that can damage eye tissues and contribute to itchiness, promoting overall eye health and well-being.

Immune-boosting properties: By stimulating the immune system, herbal wild potato capsules help combat underlying infections or allergies that may be causing itchy eyes, reducing symptoms and promoting faster healing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, roots help reduce redness and swelling associated with itchy eyes.
Rhyzomes: Containing natural antibacterial properties, rhyzomes prevent infections from contributing to itchy eye conditions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh taraxacum kok-saghyz roots and wash them thoroughly under cold running water for 30 minutes.
Dry the cleaned roots in a warm oven at 150°f for 12 hours or until they become brittle and fragile.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using an electric grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix the powdered root with 500mg of gelatin in a small bowl, adding 100ml of hot distilled water to create a uniform paste, then let it sit at room temperature for 30 minutes.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with the prepared herbal mixture using a capsule filling machine or spoon and store them in an airtight container in a cool dry place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dogwood capsules helps with itchy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated eyelids. The natural ingredients in dogwood extract reduce redness, swelling, and itchiness, providing fast relief from discomfort. Additionally, the antioxidants present in dogwood help protect the delicate tissues around the eyes from oxidative stress and damage, further reducing irritation and promoting a healthy vision. By addressing the root cause of itchy eyes, dogwood capsules bring welcome relief to individuals suffering from this common problem.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Certain herbs like chamomile, calendula, and eyebright contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce redness, swelling, and itchiness in the eyes.

Antioxidant properties: Antioxidants like vitamin C-rich herbs (e.g., rosehips) or polyphenol-rich herbs (e.g., green tea) can help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which may contribute to itchy eyes.
Soil antipruritic properties: Some herbs like licorice root or marshmallow root have soothing and anti-itching properties that can help calm irritated eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Dogwood roots have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and itching in the eyes.
Barks: The barks of the dogwood plant contain tannins, which have antiseptic and astringent properties, making them effective in soothing and calming irritated eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality cephalanthus occidentalis leaves in the early morning or late evening for maximum potency.
Clean and dry the harvested leaves thoroughly with a soft brush and air circulation for 30 minutes.
Steep 10 grams of dried cephalanthus occidentalis leaves in 100 milliliters of boiling distilled water for 15-20 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid extract.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 250 milligrams of the cephalanthus occidentalis liquid extract and seal tightly for storage and dosing.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel capsules helps with itchy eyes because they contain an active compound called anethole, which has anti-inflammatory properties. When taken as a supplement, anethole can help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the eyes, providing relief from itchy eyes caused by allergies or other irritants. The capsule's anti-inflammatory effects also help to soothe dry, irritated eyes and promote overall eye health. This natural remedy provides a safe and effective way to alleviate itchy eye discomfort and maintain healthy vision.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Fennel's antioxidants help neutralize free radicals in the body, which can contribute to inflammation and irritation in the eyes, reducing itchiness and discomfort.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in fennel capsules, such as flavonoids and terpenes, can help reduce swelling and redness in the eyes, providing relief from itchiness and irritation.
Expectorant properties: Fennel's natural expectorant qualities can help loosen and clear mucus from the eyes, reducing congestion and itchiness caused by allergies or sinus infections.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots are known for their anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing itchy eyes and reducing redness.
Seeds: Fennel seeds have natural antihistamine properties that help alleviate itchiness and discomfort in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Gather 100 grams of dried foeniculum vulgare seeds and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 500 milliliters of cold water with 50 grams of glycerin in a heat-resistant glass bowl to create a base solution.
Add the powdered foeniculum vulgare seeds to the base solution and stir for 10 minutes to ensure thorough mixing.
Pour the mixture into empty vegetarian capsules, filling each capsule to approximately 500 milligrams with a precision scale.
Store the herbal capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months or until desired dosage is reached.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Field horsetail capsules helps with itchy eyes because they contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm irritated eyes. remedy_recipe: Gather equisetum arvense dried herb, gelatin capsules, and a mortar and pestle for processing.
Weigh 500mg of equisetum arvense dried herb and grind it into a fine powder using the mortar and pestle.
Mix 250mg of the powdered equisetum arvense with 125mg of gelatin in a small bowl until well combined.
Fill 50-60 empty gelatin capsules with the herbal mixture and seal them immediately to preserve potency.
Store the finished capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to one year or refrigerate for longer storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle capsules helps with itchy eyes because they contain anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and itching. The herbal remedy works by blocking the production of histamine, a chemical released during allergic reactions, which causes itchiness and redness in the eyes. Additionally, stinging nettle's antioxidant properties help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, further reducing inflammation and discomfort. By targeting the root cause of itchy eyes, stinging nettle capsules provide effective relief for individuals suffering from seasonal allergies, dry eye syndrome, or other forms of ocular itchiness.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Stinging nettle capsules contain anti-inflammatory compounds such as flavonoids and caffeic acid, which help reduce inflammation in the eye and alleviate itchiness.

Antihistamine properties: The capsules also possess antihistamine properties, which can counteract the effects of histamine released during allergic reactions, providing relief from itchy eyes caused by allergies or irritants.

Vasoconstrictive agents: Stinging nettle contains vasoconstrictive agents that help constrict blood vessels in the eye, reducing swelling and itching associated with conjunctivitis, blepharitis, or other eye conditions.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce redness and itching associated with irritated eyes.
Seeds: Containing essential fatty acids, vitamins, and minerals, seeds provide a natural soothing effect on itchy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry high-quality urtica dioica leaves to ensure potency.
Grind 500mg of dried urtica dioica leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Mix the powdered urtica dioica with 1000mg of silica gel and 100mg of vegetable starch in a clean bowl.
Fill empty capsules with the herbal mixture, ensuring each capsule contains 1500mg total weight.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow capsules helps with itchy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated eyes. remedy_recipe: Dry aachillea millefolium leaves in a warm, well-ventilated area for 2-3 weeks.
Grind dried aachillea millefolium leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 500mg of the powdered herb with 1000mg of vegetable capsules and fill them immediately to ensure potency.
Store prepared capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months.
Take 2-3 capsules daily as needed, or as directed by a healthcare professional.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rowan capsules helps with itchy eyes because they contain natural anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm irritated eyes. remedy_recipe: Harvest dried leaves of sorbus aucuparia in amounts of 250 grams.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar.
Mix 10 grams of powdered leaves with 100 milliliters of boiling water for 15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth to remove any solid particles and filter it.
Fill gelatin capsules with 250 milligrams of the filtered liquid extract and store them.

intro_desc: Herbal capsules for itchy eyes are a natural remedy designed to soothe and calm irritated eyes caused by allergies, dryness, or other environmental factors. These capsules contain potent herbs like Eyebright, Goldenseal, and Marshmallow Root that help reduce inflammation, ease redness, and eliminate itchiness. For instance, Eyebright capsules have been known to alleviate itchy eyes caused by conjunctivitis and allergic reactions. By taking these herbal capsules regularly, people can enjoy improved eye comfort and clarity, allowing them to engage in daily activities without distraction or discomfort.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to itchy eyes that are treated with herbal capsules are conjunctivitis, dry eyes, and blepharitis. Capsules containing herbs such as eyebright, bilberry, and chamomile have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm irritated eyes. Additionally, herbs like turmeric and ginger have natural antibacterial properties that can help combat infections. These herbal capsules can provide relief from redness, itchiness, and discomfort associated with these eye conditions.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal capsules that help with itchy eyes is Eyebright and Quercetin. Eyebright, rich in flavonoids, reduces inflammation and soothes the eyes, while Quercetin's antioxidant properties combat free radicals and alleviate itchiness. These two herbs work synergistically to calm red, itchy eyes and promote healthy vision. Additionally, Licorice root, with its anti-inflammatory properties, can also be added to enhance the benefits. However, always consult a healthcare professional before taking any herbal supplements.
